description_long: This is a large commercial jet airliner with a wide fuselage and a distinctive hump at the front, characteristic of the Boeing 747 family. The airplane features four underwing turbofan engines, two on each wing, and a low-wing configuration with swept-back wings. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is extended, showing a tricycle arrangement with two main gear units and a nose gear. The exterior is primarily white with a small red and blue accent on the tail fin, and the surface appears smooth and metallic.
description: A large, white commercial jet airliner with four underwing engines, swept low wings, and a distinctive hump at the front. The airplane has a tricycle landing gear and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, four engines, and the airplane's wide fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, landing gear, and the distinctive upper deck hump.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the engine arrangement.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to view 1, showing the landing gear, engines, and tail fin with colored accents.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with moderate dihedral angle and no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows.
